CONTENT.Honours: San LorenzoPrimera División: 2007 ClausuraEstudiantes de La PlataPrimera División: 2010 AperturaBoca JuniorsPrimera División: 2011 Apertura, 2015 Argentine Primera DivisiónCopa Argentina: 2011–12, 2015Colo-ColoPrimera División: Transición 2017Supercopa de Chile: 2017 Supercopa de Chile, 2018 Supercopa de ChileArgentinaFIFA World Cup runner-up: 2014Copa America runner-up: 2007Ubaldo Fillol Award: 2011 Apertura (with Boca Juniors)
CONTENT.Club career: Orion was San Lorenzo's first choice goalkeeper in 2007, when he helped the club to win the Clausura tournament.On 9 December 2009, the goalkeeper left San Lorenzo for 500 thousand euros and joined Estudiantes de La Plata.In July 2011 Orion signed with Boca Juniors, where he won the 2011 Apertura. In 2013, Agustín received offers from important European clubs, but he decided to stay in Boca Juniors, because he is an important piece in the team and he loves the club.In August 2016 Orion signed with Racing Club.
CONTENT.External links: Argentine Primera statistics at Fútbol XXI (in Spanish)Agustín Orión at National-Football-Teams.comAgustín Orión at Soccerway
CONTENT.International career: Orion was called up to the Argentina national football team as third goalkeeper behind Roberto Abbondanzieri and Juan Pablo Carrizo for the 2007 Copa América held in Venezuela.He made his debut for the Albiceleste against Brazil in the Superclásico de las Américas in September 2011.In June 2014, Orion was named in Argentina's squad for the 2014 FIFA World Cup.

SUMMARY: Agustín Ignacio Orión (Spanish pronunciation: [aɣusˈtin oˈɾjon]; born 26 June 1981) is an Argentine former professional footballer who played as a goalkeeper. He was capped three times for Argentina.
